recommendations: define and run a minimal MCP smoke-test suite (import + one tiny RHF/RKS calculation) in CI since test status is empty
add strict input/output schemas (Pydantic models) for each exposed endpoint to prevent ambiguous argument passing for overloaded names like kernel/analyze
de-duplicate and namespace MCP endpoint names (e.g.
scf.rhf.kernel vs cc.ccsd.kernel) to avoid collisions in tool routing
add timeout/memory guards and “small-system only” defaults for expensive methods (CCSD/CASSCF/PBC) to reduce runaway jobs
create a curated “safe endpoint allowlist” for MCP (core stable APIs only) and move experimental/high-cost functions behind an explicit opt-in flag
implement structured error mapping (PySCF exceptions → MCP error codes with actionable hints) for better client reliability
pin and document compatibility matrix across Python/PySCF/numpy/scipy/h5py plus optional deps (xcfun/libxc/mpi4py) to reduce environment drift
add deterministic example-based regression tests for key workflows (Mole→RHF→MP2
Mole→RKS
Cell→KRHF) with numerical tolerances
add observability hooks (per-call timing
method metadata
molecule size
convergence status) and optional tracing IDs in MCP responses
improve README_MCP with “quickstart + limits + cost warnings + supported methods” and copy-paste client examples
add caching/reuse guidance for checkpoint files and integrals to avoid repeated expensive setup in interactive MCP usage
introduce async job handling or background-task mode for long-running computations with polling/cancel support
harden serialization boundaries (explicit unit conventions
ndarray shape validation
complex-number handling) for qcschema and dumps/loads paths
run lint/type checks on generated MCP plugin code (ruff + mypy/pyright) and enforce in CI
add security guardrails for user-provided file paths and external data loading (sandbox/allowlist
no arbitrary file traversal)

success_factors: End-to-end workflow status is success with all planned nodes executed (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Import-based adapter strategy is appropriate for PySCF’s library-first architecture and showed high feasibility (0.9).
MCP plugin started healthy over stdio, indicating basic runtime viability.
Repository ingestion succeeded despite fallback path (zip fallback), preserving continuity.
failure_reasons: Validation depth is limited: original project tests were not effectively executed (passed=false with empty details, 0 execution time).
DeepWiki enrichment failed, reducing external contextual analysis quality.
Observability/resource metrics were not captured (memory/cpu/throughput all zero), limiting performance confidence.

node_performance.download_time: Completed successfully; exact per-node timing not provided. Repo ingestion used zip fallback, which likely increased I/O overhead but remained reliable.
node_performance.analysis_time: Completed successfully for a very large codebase (1593 files; 35 packages surfaced). Reasonable given complexity; no blocking errors.
node_performance.generation_time: Completed successfully with 7 plugin artifacts generated; however reported LOC/size as 0 suggests metrics instrumentation gap, not generation failure.
node_performance.test_time: MCP health check succeeded quickly, but substantive test execution appears absent (0s/empty results), so true functional test time is effectively not measured.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Unknown due to missing telemetry (reported 0 MB). Efficiency cannot be validated.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Likely moderate-high because PySCF is large and artifact-rich; generated plugin footprint appears small, but reported size=0 indicates measurement gap.
